item.title: Japanese Firms Reduce Share Buybacks for First Time Since 2020
item.description: Japanese firms announced fewer share buyback programs in the fiscal year ended Tuesday, marking the first decline since 2020.

item.title: Blue Owl Reels as Investors Who Fueled Its Growth Now Want Out
item.description: The top-line numbers from Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s funds were unambiguous and, by just about any measure, ugly.

item.title: Asian Equities May Open Higher After US Rebound: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks may open with modest gains after US shares closed higher on hopes the Iran war is slowly moving toward a resolution. Trading is set to be muted with a number of regional markets shut for holidays.

item.title: Broadcom Taps Google Executive Thuener as Its Next Finance Chief
item.description: Broadcom Inc., one of the most valuable publicly traded semiconductor companies, named Alphabet Inc. executive Amie Thuener as its next chief financial officer, replacing the retiring Kirsten Spears.

item.title: British Columbia Gets Fifth Credit Downgrade From S&P Since 2021
item.description: British Columbia, the western Canadian province that once boasted a top AAA credit rating, was downgraded by S&P Global Ratings for the fifth time since 2021 on Thursday.

item.title: SpaceX’s Record Listing Could Kick Off a Year of Massive AI IPOs
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic are also considering a move to the public markets

item.title: Wall Street Money Managers Hunker Down as Iran War Rages On
item.description: Five weeks of war with Iran have wiped trillions off global stocks, pushed oil past $100 and kept it there and repriced wagers on interest rates and inflation.

item.title: US Blue-Chip Bond Funds See $5.3 Billion Outflow, Most in a Year
item.description: US investment-grade bond funds suffered the largest weekly outflows in about a year as rising macro-economic risk led to significant losses for the asset class.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Sees Risks to Inflation, Employment Balanced
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said risks to inflation and employment from higher energy prices are “in balance” and that he favors holding interest rates steady.

item.title: SpaceX Targets More Than $2 Trillion Valuation in IPO
item.description: SpaceX boosted its target IPO valuation above $2 trillion, according to people familiar with the matter, as the world’s most valuable startup gears up to pitch potentially the biggest-ever market debut.

item.title: Trump Restructures Broad Metals Tariffs But Keeps 50% Rate
item.description: The Trump administration said it will maintain 50% tariffs on many imported steel, aluminum and copper products, even as it moves to simplify duties for goods made with negligible amounts of the metals.

item.title: Microsoft Hit ‘Audacious’ Copilot Goals After Analyst Input
item.description: Microsoft Corp., responding to Wall Street feedback, has pivoted its AI sales strategy to focus on selling Copilot rather than offering it for free as part of a software bundle.

item.title: Canada’s Irving Turns to Newfoundland for Oil on Iran Disruption
item.description: Canada’s biggest refinery is turning to Newfoundland for crude for the first time since 2020 as the war on Iran cuts off Middle Eastern supplies.

item.title: Ex-Centerview Banker Gets Deal to End US Insider-Trading Case
item.description: Federal prosecutors have reached a settlement to eventually drop criminal charges against a former Centerview Partners LLC investment banker who was accused of participating in an international insider-trading ring and has been living in her native Thailand for years.

item.title: Tower Operator SBA Communications Is Exploring a Potential Sale
item.description: SBA Communications Corp. is exploring options including a potential sale after the tower owner and operator received preliminary takeover interest, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Three Ships Appear to Enter Hormuz by New Route Along Oman Coast
item.description: Three tankers broadcasting Omani ownership appeared to enter the Strait of Hormuz by hugging their home country’s coastline, indicating a different route to a northerly path through Iranian waters.

item.title: OpenAI Buys Tech Talk Show TBPN in Rare Move Into Media
item.description: OpenAI has acquired the online technology talk show TBPN, an unusual purchase for the artificial intelligence model developer aimed at driving the conversation around AI.

item.title: Harold Hamm’s Continental Is Boosting Oil Output as Prices Soar
item.description: Billionaire oil wildcatter Harold Hamm’s Continental Resources Inc. plans to increase production as the war in Iran sends crude prices soaring to the highest in four years.

item.title: Podcast Companies Are Looking a Lot Like Record Labels
item.description: Like in music, the power is shifting toward hosts of popular shows and nimble networks who are building technology to support their work.

item.title: Brazil’s Overlooked Small Caps Are Starting to Appear Cheap
item.description: A flood of foreign money into Brazil’s biggest companies in the first quarter has left small caps trading at their cheapest versus larger peers in over six years, with some investors saying the shift has gone too far.

item.title: Oaktree BDC Chief Sees ‘Excessive Risk-Taking’ in Private Credit
item.description: Software-sector weakness, liquidity concerns and a series of bad underwriting vintages are all widening the moat between public and non-traded private credit vehicles, according to Oaktree Capital Management’s Armen Panossian.

item.title: Hedge Funds Bail From Global Stocks at Fastest Pace in 13 Years
item.description: Fast-money investors are rushing to unwind their global equity exposure amid diminishing hopes for a swift resolution of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: FBI Calls Breach of Sensitive Agency Networks a ‘Major Incident’
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation has concluded that last month’s breach of the networks it uses to manage wiretaps and other surveillance work qualifies as a “major incident,” signaling the severity of an intrusion that had already prompted the agency to launch a criminal probe and move to toughen cybersecurity.

item.title: India AI Startup Sarvam Raises Funds at $1.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: India’s homegrown artificial intelligence startup, Sarvam AI, is close to raising $300 million to $350 million as it seeks to build a domestic player that can compete with leaders from the US and China.

item.title: Fund Founded by Pritzkers Inks Private Credit Deal for PLZ
item.description: A cleaning-products maker backed by an investment fund founded by the billionaire Pritzker family has turned to private credit lenders to refinance debt.

item.title: Amazon Imposes 3.5% Fuel Surcharge for Many Online Merchants
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. will start charging sellers who use its shipping services a 3.5% “fuel and logistics” surcharge later this month, joining the ranks of shipping companies raising prices as the war in Iran pushes oil prices higher.

item.title: Aegea Bonds Hit as Traders Sell First, Ask Later in Brazil Rout
item.description: In a matter of weeks, Aegea Saneamento e Participacoes SA went from eyeing a multi-billion-dollar valuation in a Brazilian IPO to watching its bond investors rush for the exits.

item.title: NASA Astronauts Gearing Up for Final Engine Push in Path to Moon
item.description: A NASA crew of four astronauts are preparing for a critical stage of their journey that will set them on course for the moon, a step that will ultimately bring them closer to the lunar surface than anyone has been in more than 50 years.

item.title: US Lawmakers Propose Crackdown on Sales of Chip Tools to China
item.description: US lawmakers unveiled legislation that seeks to crack down on exports of chipmaking tools to China, especially from allies including the Netherlands and Japan, in a bipartisan effort to help Washington further constrain Beijing’s technology ambitions.

item.title: Nordstrom’s Sales Return to 2019 Levels After Deal to Go Private
item.description: Nordstrom’s revenue has returned to its pre-pandemic peak less than a year after the department store went private in a $6.25 billion deal, according to people familiar with the matter.
